---
title: "00 · Introduction"
subtitle: "Tier MFRL — Mathematics for Reinforcement Learning · 40 lessons deriving every modern RL algorithm from the Bellman equation"
---

## What this tier is for

This tier walks Shiyu Zhao's *Mathematical Foundations of Reinforcement
Learning* (Springer 2025) lesson by lesson. Unlike the seven synthesis
tiers — which give you the *math* — this tier shows how the math
*combines* into the algorithms that drive modern RL.

By the end of the tier you can:

- **Define an MDP** and derive its **Bellman equation** in both
  scalar and matrix-vector form.
- **Solve a small MDP** in closed form via $v = (I - \gamma P_\pi)^{-1} r_\pi$.
- **Prove** that the Bellman optimality equation has a unique
  solution, via the contraction-mapping theorem.
- **Implement** value iteration, policy iteration, and truncated PI
  from first principles.
- **Run model-free** Monte Carlo control with exploring starts or ε-greedy.
- **Understand** Robbins-Monro and Dvoretzky as the convergence
  engines of every iterative RL method.
- **Derive** TD(0), Sarsa, n-step Sarsa, Q-learning, and expected
  Sarsa as one update with different targets.
- **Recognise the deadly triad** (bootstrapping × off-policy × FA)
  and how DQN tames it.
- **Derive** the policy gradient theorem and write down REINFORCE.
- **Understand** A2C, off-policy AC, and the deterministic PG (DDPG)
  as the modern actor-critic family.

## How to read this tier

**Strict prerequisite chain.** Each lesson depends on the previous
one by design. The prerequisite graph in `_curriculum.yml` is the
source of truth — if a lesson links to a synthesis prerequisite
(e.g. `t5.matrices` for the matrix-vector Bellman lesson), that
external prereq is also required.

**Read alongside Zhao's PDF.** [Free download](https://github.com/MathFoundationRL/Book-Mathematical-Foundation-of-Reinforcement-Learning) of every chapter from the author's GitHub. This tier adds the
*Touch* widget, the *Wonder* questions, the graduated hints, the
glossary cross-links, and the prerequisite scaffolding Zhao's
compactness leaves to the reader.

**Compare with the Tier 7 sister synthesis lesson** ([`t7.rl-theory`](../tier-7-masters-tracks/06-rl-theory/index.qmd))
when you want the high-level take. Tier 7 gives you the
single-paragraph view; this tier gives you the full derivation.

## The lessons by part

```{mermaid}
flowchart LR
    A[Part 1: Tools<br/>L01-22<br/>MDPs through SGD]
    B[Part 2: Algorithms<br/>L23-40<br/>TD through DDPG]
    A --> B
```

### Part 1 — Basic Tools (lessons 1-22)

#### Chapter 1 — Basic Concepts

1. [**01 · What is an MDP? Grid-World Foundations**](01-mdp-and-grid-world/index.qmd)
2. [**02 · State Transitions and the Markov Property**](02-state-transitions/index.qmd)
3. [**03 · Policies as Conditional Distributions**](03-policy/index.qmd)
4. [**04 · Trajectories, Returns, and the Discount**](04-trajectories-returns-episodes/index.qmd)

#### Chapter 2 — State Values and Bellman Equation

5. [**05 · State Values: Expected Return**](05-state-values/index.qmd)
6. [**06 · The Bellman Equation, Derived**](06-bellman-equation/index.qmd)
7. [**07 · Matrix-Vector Bellman and Closed-Form Solution**](07-bellman-matrix-form/index.qmd)
8. [**08 · From State Values to Action Values**](08-action-values/index.qmd)

#### Chapter 3 — Optimal Values and the BOE

9. [**09 · Optimal Policies and Optimal Values**](09-optimal-policies/index.qmd)
10. [**10 · The Bellman Optimality Equation (BOE)**](10-bellman-optimality-equation/index.qmd)
11. [**11 · The Banach Fixed-Point Theorem**](11-contraction-mapping-theorem/index.qmd)
12. [**12 · The BOE is a Contraction; v\* is Its Unique Fixed Point**](12-boe-uniqueness/index.qmd)

#### Chapter 4 — Value and Policy Iteration

13. [**13 · Value Iteration**](13-value-iteration/index.qmd)
14. [**14 · Policy Iteration**](14-policy-iteration/index.qmd)
15. [**15 · Truncated Policy Iteration**](15-truncated-policy-iteration/index.qmd)

#### Chapter 5 — Monte Carlo Methods

16. [**16 · Mean Estimation from Samples**](16-mean-estimation-from-samples/index.qmd)
17. [**17 · MC Basic: Model-Free Policy Iteration**](17-mc-basic/index.qmd)
18. [**18 · Sample Efficiency: Every-Visit MC and Exploring Starts**](18-mc-exploring-starts/index.qmd)
19. [**19 · ε-Greedy: Exploration without Exploring Starts**](19-epsilon-greedy/index.qmd)

#### Chapter 6 — Stochastic Approximation

20. [**20 · Robbins-Monro: Solving g(w)=0 from Noisy Samples**](20-robbins-monro/index.qmd)
21. [**21 · Dvoretzky's Convergence Theorem**](21-dvoretzky-convergence/index.qmd)
22. [**22 · SGD: Gradient Descent with Sampled Gradients**](22-stochastic-gradient-descent/index.qmd)

### Part 2 — Algorithms (lessons 23-40)

#### Chapter 7 — Temporal-Difference Methods

23. [**23 · TD(0): Bootstrapped Value Learning**](23-td-state-values/index.qmd)
24. [**24 · Sarsa: TD for Action Values, On-Policy**](24-sarsa/index.qmd)
25. [**25 · n-Step Sarsa: Bridging TD(0) and Monte Carlo**](25-n-step-sarsa/index.qmd)
26. [**26 · Q-Learning: TD for Optimal Action Values, Off-Policy**](26-q-learning/index.qmd)
27. [**27 · TD Methods Unified: Sarsa, Q, Expected Sarsa**](27-td-unified-view/index.qmd)

#### Chapter 8 — Value Function Methods

28. [**28 · From Table to Function: Why Function Approximation?**](28-from-table-to-function/index.qmd)
29. [**29 · TD with Function Approximation: Semi-Gradient TD**](29-td-with-function-approximation/index.qmd)
30. [**30 · Q-Learning with FA, and the Deadly Triad**](30-q-learning-with-fa/index.qmd)
31. [**31 · Deep Q-Network: Target Networks and Replay Buffers**](31-deep-q-network/index.qmd)
32. [**32 · The Projected Bellman Operator and MSPBE**](32-projected-bellman-operator/index.qmd)
33. [**33 · Gradient-TD: GTD, GTD2, and TDC**](33-gradient-td/index.qmd)

#### Chapter 9 — Policy Gradient Methods

34. [**34 · Parameterising Policies: Softmax and Gaussian**](34-policy-parameterization/index.qmd)
35. [**35 · The Policy Gradient Theorem**](35-policy-gradient-theorem/index.qmd)
36. [**36 · REINFORCE: Monte Carlo Policy Gradient**](36-reinforce/index.qmd)

#### Chapter 10 — Actor-Critic Methods

37. [**37 · QAC: The Simplest Actor-Critic**](37-actor-critic-qac/index.qmd)
38. [**38 · A2C: Advantage Estimation and Baseline Invariance**](38-advantage-actor-critic/index.qmd)
39. [**39 · Off-Policy Actor-Critic and Importance Sampling**](39-off-policy-actor-critic/index.qmd)
40. [**40 · Deterministic Policy Gradient (DPG) and DDPG**](40-deterministic-actor-critic/index.qmd)

## Track complete

All 40 lessons are authored against the six-beat contract — every
lesson has the required callouts (fun-fact, key-insight,
common-confusion, Takeaways, Self-Assessment), graduated hints on
every exercise, and a sister-synthesis link to
[`t7.rl-theory`](../tier-7-masters-tracks/06-rl-theory/index.qmd).

Track-level glossary contributions: 62 RL-specific terms added to
the [site-wide glossary](../../glossary.qmd) (markov-decision-process,
state-value, action-value, bellman-optimality-equation,
contraction-mapping, banach-fixed-point-theorem, value-iteration,
policy-iteration, monte-carlo-estimation, robbins-monro-algorithm,
td-error, sarsa, q-learning, value-function-approximation,
deadly-triad, dqn, projected-bellman-operator, mspbe, gradient-td,
tdc, policy-gradient-theorem, reinforce, actor-critic, ddpg, …).

::: {.callout-note}
This page reflects the snapshot of `_curriculum.yml` at the time of
build. Lesson statuses there are the source of truth.
:::
